topic: Chernobyl 1986: How a Safety Test Killed 31 and Poisoned Europe
hook: A safety test caused the worst nuclear disaster
key_points: April 26 1986 reactor 4 of Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant exploded at 1:23 AM
Cause: positive void coefficient design flaw in RBMK-1000 plus operator violations
31 immediate deaths from blast and acute radiation syndrome
INSAG-7 1992 attributed cause to reactor design and safety culture failure
AZ-5 emergency shutdown button triggered the explosion due to graphite tip rods
350000 people evacuated 30km exclusion zone still active
Led to global nuclear safety overhaul and IAEA Convention on Nuclear Safety 1994

topic: Bhopal 1984: The Worst Industrial Disaster in History Killed 25000
hook: A leaking valve killed 25000 in one night
key_points: December 2-3 1984 Union Carbide India pesticide plant released 40 tons of methyl isocyanate
Estimated 3787 immediate deaths official rising to 25000 over decades
MIC tank E610 contaminated with water triggered runaway exothermic reaction
Refrigeration system disabled to save costs flare tower under maintenance
Warren Anderson CEO arrested then released bail jumped never extradited
Union Carbide settled $470 million in 1989 inadequate per victims
Bhopal Gas Tragedy birthed global Right-to-Know laws

topic: Tacoma Narrows Bridge 1940: How Wind Made a Bridge Dance to Death
hook: Wind made a brand new bridge dance apart
key_points: November 7 1940 Tacoma Narrows Bridge collapsed at 11 AM in 42 mph winds
Bridge opened only 4 months earlier July 1 1940
Aeroelastic flutter from torsional oscillation caused collapse not pure resonance
Designer Leon Moisseiff used shallow plate girder instead of trusses for elegance
Tubby the cocker spaniel only fatality couldn't be rescued from car
Famous footage by Barney Elliott still studied in engineering schools
Led to mandatory wind tunnel testing for all major bridges

topic: Three Mile Island 1979: The Meltdown That Almost Killed Pennsylvania
hook: America came one valve from nuclear catastrophe
key_points: March 28 1979 reactor 2 of TMI-2 partial meltdown began at 4 AM
PORV pilot operated relief valve stuck open releasing coolant
Operators misread instruments and shut down emergency cooling pumps
60% of reactor core melted but pressure vessel contained fuel
Released 13 million curies of radioactive gases mainly krypton-85
Kemeny Commission 1979 cited operator error and poor instrumentation
Public confidence in nuclear power devastated cancelled 51 reactor plans

topic: Texas City Refinery 2005: How BP's Penny-Pinching Killed 15 Workers
hook: BP saved millions and killed 15 workers
key_points: March 23 2005 explosion at BP Texas City refinery killed 15 injured 180

CSB report 2007 cited cost-cutting deferred maintenance and trailer placement
Blowdown drum without flare last replaced 1950s outdated design
Baker Panel chaired by James Baker found systemic safety culture failure
BP fined $87 million by OSHA largest in agency history at time
Triggered industry-wide review of process safety management

topic: Hindenburg 1937: The 36-Second Inferno That Killed Airship Travel Forever

key_points: May 6 1937 LZ 129 Hindenburg burst into flames at Lakehurst New Jersey 7:25 PM
Killed 35 of 97 aboard plus 1 ground crew member 36 seconds total
Likely cause: hydrogen leak ignited by static discharge during landing
Aluminum-doped fabric covering also flammable contributed to spread
Herbert Morrison's radio broadcast 'Oh the humanity!' became iconic
Helium would have prevented disaster but US embargoed export to Nazi Germany
Ended commercial passenger airship era replaced by airplanes

topic: Fukushima Daiichi 2011: When a Tsunami Defeated Three Nuclear Reactors

INES Level 7 maximum on International Nuclear Event Scale
470000 evacuated Japan suspended all nuclear power until 2015 restart
TEPCO and government criticized for slow response and information control

topic: Challenger 1986: How a $900 Rubber Ring Killed 7 Astronauts

key_points: January 28 1986 Space Shuttle Challenger broke apart 73 seconds after launch

NASA management overruled engineering concerns to maintain launch schedule

Led to creation of NASA Office of Safety and Mission Assurance
